Tabernaemontana simulans is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is part of the order Gentianales and the clade Asterids. The genus Tabernaemontana is native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. Within the Apocynaceae family, Tabernaemontana simulans is one of the many species found in the genus Tabernaemontana.

intro: Tabernaemontana simulans is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the "wild coffee" or "yellow tabernaemontana". The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antihypertensive properties. The bioactive constituents of Tabernaemontana simulans include alkaloids, such as voacangine, voacristine, and voacamine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat conditions such as fever, headache, and skin infections.
